macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh, Trump's nominee to the Federal Reserve (likely as Chair or Governor), cleared a key Senate procedural vote, putting him on track for confirmation.
primary_drivers: Warsh is historically hawkish on inflation/balance sheet but dovish on regulation, suggesting lighter bank supervision
Market perception of Fed independence and reaction function shifting
Potential steeper yield curve as Warsh has criticized large Fed balance sheet (long-end yields up, less cuts priced)

beneficiaries.rationale: Warsh is a former Morgan Stanley banker known for favoring lighter bank regulation and capital rules; large-cap investment banks benefit most from anticipated rollback of Basel III endgame and supervisory leniency.
Same regulatory rollback thesis — Warsh's own former employer; capital markets-heavy banks gain operating leverage from looser capital requirements and freer trading/IB activity.
Steeper curve (Warsh has publicly criticized Fed balance sheet, implying QT/higher long rates) directly improves net interest margin on cash sweeps and lending book.
Jamie Dimon has publicly praised Warsh; megabank capital relief and friendlier stress-test regime under a Warsh Fed would directly free up buybacks.

headlines: Trump Fed pick Kevin Warsh clears key Senate hurdle, teeing up final vote

macro_events.event_summary: Defense Secretary Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Caine testify before Congress on Pentagon budget amid an active Iran war, signaling elevated/sustained defense spending.
primary_drivers: Elevated defense appropriations and supplemental funding tied to active Iran conflict
Munitions/missile replenishment demand from ongoing strikes
Oil supply risk premium from Middle East conflict

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest pure defense prime; PAC-3, THAAD, JASSM, and F-35 sustainment all directly consumed/expended in an Iran conflict. Supplemental budget testimony typically precedes munitions reorders.
Maker of Standard Missile family (SM-2/3/6) and Tomahawk — the primary munitions being expended by Navy assets in CENTCOM. Direct replenishment beneficiary of any supplemental.
L3Harris supplies tactical comms, EW, and small-munitions (via Aerojet acquisition — solid rocket motors). SRM bottleneck is exactly what supplemental funding targets.
B-21, Global Hawk, and IBCS air-defense integrator; direct exposure to standoff strike and missile-defense buildouts in CENTCOM theater.
Combat systems (munitions/artillery via OTS) and Virginia-class subs launching Tomahawks; secondary munitions replenishment play plus shipbuilding budget protection.
Target drones, hypersonic test vehicles, and tactical UAS — small-cap with outsized sensitivity to supplemental defense bills and rapid-acquisition language often discussed in war-time testimony.
Iran war = Strait of Hormuz risk premium on Brent; integrated major with low Iran/Gulf production exposure benefits from higher realized crude with limited disruption risk.
Maven Smart System and AI targeting contracts directly used in current CENTCOM operations; budget testimony amid active war reinforces software-procurement narrative.

losers.rationale: Mideast war + sustained higher jet fuel prices compress airline margins; Delta has highest international long-haul exposure among US carriers and no fuel hedges.
Heavy transpacific/Atlantic routing requires Gulf airspace avoidance, raising fuel burn and block times; fuel cost passthrough is the dominant headwind.
Cruise operator with Mediterranean/Mideast itineraries; war headlines depress booking curves and force itinerary changes — direct demand hit.
Discretionary retailer squeezed by gasoline-price drag on low/mid-income consumer wallet share if oil sustains a war premium.

headlines: Hegseth, Caine testifying on Pentagon budget amid Iran war

macro_events.event_summary: Iran war is driving a Treasury bond selloff (yields rising) ahead of Powell's final FOMC press conference, combining geopolitical oil/defense risk with hawkish rate pressure.
primary_drivers: Middle East war premium lifting crude prices
Higher long-end Treasury yields raising discount rates and pressuring duration-sensitive assets
Defense spending tailwind from active Iran conflict

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct beneficiary of higher crude prices from Iran war premium; integrated major with significant upstream leverage to Brent.
Pure-play US E&P with high oil-price beta; war premium directly lifts realized prices and FCF.
Active Iran conflict accelerates missile/munitions restocking (PAC-3, THAAD, JASSM) — Lockheed is the prime contractor for the most-used systems in Middle East theater.
Raytheon makes Patriot, SM-3, Tomahawk — all consumed at high rates in Israel/Gulf air defense and strike operations against Iran.
B-2/B-21 strategic bomber and ICBM exposure; Iran war elevates strategic deterrence budget priority.
Higher long-end yields steepen the curve and expand NIM for the largest US bank with significant asset-sensitive balance sheet.
Pure-play upstream with oil-weighted production; strong leverage to Brent spike from Hormuz/Iran risk.
Oilfield services see capex reacceleration when crude rallies on supply shock; Halliburton has high North America completion leverage.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25% of airline opex; Iran war crude spike directly compresses margins, and Delta has limited fuel hedging vs peers.
Same jet fuel cost passthrough; United also has heavier international/Middle East routing exposure to airspace closures and rerouting costs.
Southwest's all-737 domestic model has high fuel sensitivity and reduced pricing power vs legacies to pass through fuel costs.
Realty Income — bond proxy REIT with long-duration lease cash flows; rising long yields directly compress valuation multiple.
Carnival has high fuel cost exposure plus Mediterranean/Middle East itinerary risk from active Iran war forcing rerouting.

headlines: Iran war is fueling a bond selloff ahead of Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s final press conference

macro_events.event_summary: US housing starts surged to a 15-month high, signaling a pickup in new home construction even as the broader real-estate market remains weak.
primary_drivers: Higher new-home construction volume drives demand for builder products, building materials, and appliances
Divergence between new construction (strong) and existing home sales (weak) favors homebuilders over realtors/brokerages

beneficiaries.rationale: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder by volume; new housing starts directly translate to revenue/closings. Pure-play exposure.
Lennar is the #2 US homebuilder; starts data is a leading indicator for its delivery volumes and order book.
PulteGroup is a top-5 builder with broad geographic exposure; benefits directly from accelerating starts.
Builders FirstSource sells lumber, trusses, and millwork directly into single-family construction — revenue is a near-direct function of housing starts.
Eagle Materials supplies wallboard and cement consumed per housing start; volume leverage to new construction.
Whirlpool ships appliances into new homes (built-in installations during construction); higher starts feed appliance demand 2-4 months out.
Masco's faucets, cabinets, and paint are installed during new home construction; higher starts boost OEM channel volumes.
Home Depot's pro segment serves builders and subcontractors; rising starts increase pro-channel volumes, though existing-home weakness offsets DIY remodel demand.

losers.rationale: Zillow's revenue is tied to existing-home transactions and agent advertising; the 'slump isn't over' framing on resale market is a direct headwind despite strong starts.
Compass is pure-play residential brokerage on existing homes; doesn't benefit from new construction starts and remains pressured by the ongoing resale slump.

headlines: Construction on new homes jumps to a 15-month high, but the real-estate slump isn’t over
